\doxysubsection{Detailed Description}
Models a plane wave as an incident field in homogeneous or layered media. 

Definition at line \mbox{\hyperlink{PlaneWave_8h_source_l00021}{21}} of file \mbox{\hyperlink{PlaneWave_8h_source}{Plane\+Wave.\+h}}.

Plane\+Wave\+::\+Plane\+Wave (\begin{DoxyParamCaption}\item[{\mbox{\hyperlink{globals_8h_a52196b4b274b585226e0614490f7defc}{dcmplx}}}]{wvl}{, }\item[{double}]{eps}{, }\item[{\mbox{\hyperlink{globals_8h_ae4271193b04e5d22237184d4fad54fb0}{rvec}}}]{dir}{, }\item[{\mbox{\hyperlink{globals_8h_a876530d0f199e8f774203e2998d14f7f}{cvec}}}]{po}{}\end{DoxyParamCaption})}



Construct a homogeneous plane wave. 


\begin{DoxyParams}{Parameters}
{\em wvl} & Incident wavelength. \\
\hline
{\em eps} & Permittivity of the homogeneous medium. \\
\hline
{\em dir} & Propagation direction (3D vector). \\
\hline
{\em po} & Polarization vector.\\
\hline
\end{DoxyParams}
Normalizes the input propagation direction, sets the wavenumber $\mathbf{k} = \frac{2\pi}{\lambda}\sqrt{\varepsilon_r}\,\hat{\mathbf{d}}$, the medium impedance $ z = Z_0/\sqrt{\varepsilon_r}$, and stores polarization. 

Definition at line \mbox{\hyperlink{PlaneWave_8cpp_source_l00014}{14}} of file \mbox{\hyperlink{PlaneWave_8cpp_source}{Plane\+Wave.\+cpp}}.

Construct a plane wave in a layered medium. 


\begin{DoxyParams}{Parameters}
{\em k\+\_\+L} & Wave numbers per layer. \\
\hline
{\em epsL} & Permittivity values per layer. \\
\hline
{\em muL} & Permeability values per layer. \\
\hline
{\em z\+Vals\+Interfaces} & z-\/coordinates of interfaces. \\
\hline
{\em thickness} & Thicknesses of layers. \\
\hline
{\em prop\+Dir} & Propagation direction. \\
\hline
{\em po} & Polarization vector.\\
\hline
\end{DoxyParams}
Allocates a \doxylink{classLayeredMediaUtils}{Layered\+Media\+Utils} helper and initializes propagation parameters depending on whether the wave impinges from +z (top half-\/space) or −z (bottom). Also computes the transverse wavenumber magnitude $k_\rho$.

Computes the square root of a complex number with proper handling of negative imaginary parts. 

This function ensures correct computation of the square root for cases where the imaginary part is negative or zero to maintain consistency in branch cuts.


\begin{DoxyParams}{Parameters}
{\em arg} & The input complex number. \\
\hline
\end{DoxyParams}
\begin{DoxyReturn}{Returns}
The square root of the input complex number. 
\end{DoxyReturn}

\mbox{\hyperlink{globals_8h_a876530d0f199e8f774203e2998d14f7f}{cvec}} Plane\+Wave\+::\+EvaluateH (\begin{DoxyParamCaption}\item[{\mbox{\hyperlink{globals_8h_ae4271193b04e5d22237184d4fad54fb0}{rvec}}}]{r}{}\end{DoxyParamCaption})\hspace{0.3cm}{\ttfamily [virtual]}}



Evaluate the magnetic field at a given position (homogeneous). 


\begin{DoxyParams}{Parameters}
{\em r} & Observation point. \\
\hline
\end{DoxyParams}
\begin{DoxyReturn}{Returns}
Magnetic field vector at the point.
\end{DoxyReturn}
Uses $\mathbf{H}(\mathbf{r}) = \frac{1}{z}\,\hat{\mathbf{k}}\times\mathbf{p}\,e^{j\mathbf{k}\cdot\mathbf{r}}$. 

Implements \mbox{\hyperlink{classIncidentField_a7f960fdcdeaf381ad12a49c190f180ff}{Incident\+Field}}.

\Hypertarget{classPlaneWave_ade82a6ff2d60f8df4553d51837525a1c}\index{PlaneWave@{PlaneWave}!EvaluateLayeredEandH@{EvaluateLayeredEandH}}
\index{EvaluateLayeredEandH@{EvaluateLayeredEandH}!PlaneWave@{PlaneWave}}
\doxysubsubsection{\texorpdfstring{EvaluateLayeredEandH()}{EvaluateLayeredEandH()}}
{\footnotesize\ttfamily \label{classPlaneWave_ade82a6ff2d60f8df4553d51837525a1c} 
std\+::vector$<$ \mbox{\hyperlink{globals_8h_a876530d0f199e8f774203e2998d14f7f}{cvec}} $>$ Plane\+Wave\+::\+Evaluate\+Layered\+EandH (\begin{DoxyParamCaption}\item[{\mbox{\hyperlink{globals_8h_ae4271193b04e5d22237184d4fad54fb0}{rvec}}}]{r}{}\end{DoxyParamCaption})\hspace{0.3cm}{\ttfamily [virtual]}}



Evaluate the electric and magnetic fields at a given position in layered media. 


\begin{DoxyParams}{Parameters}
{\em r} & Observation point. \\
\hline
\end{DoxyParams}
\begin{DoxyReturn}{Returns}
Vector containing electric and magnetic field complex vectors at the point.
\end{DoxyReturn}
Decomposes the incident polarization into local TE/\+TM unit vectors, computes incident amplitudes, queries \doxylink{classLayeredMediaUtils_a6d538561bc5345114e83f7939247866f}{Layered\+Media\+Utils\+::\+Secondary} to obtain up/down "{}secondary"{} wave amplitudes within the observation layer, and assembles the fields with impedance scaling in that layer. If the observation layer equals the incidence layer, the direct incident contribution is added on top of the secondary fields. 

Implements \mbox{\hyperlink{classIncidentField_a2fbb18ff29205e11b3c0bff676b15a7e}{Incident\+Field}}.
